revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|12=draft|12=draft}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' (少初位) was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=draft|12=draft}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|12=draft|12=draft}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|ja|メンフィス|12=draft|12=draft}}, it represented the most important funerary festival in the {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|12=draft|12=draft}} necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=draft|12=draft}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|12=draft|12=draft}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the {{ill|Annals|ja|年代記|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of {{ill|Memphite|ja|メンフィス|12=draft|12=draft}} tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|12=draft|12=draft}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=draft|12=draft}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|12=draft|12=draft}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|12=draft|12=draft}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=draft|12=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=draft|12=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|12=draft|12=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|12=draft|12=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|12=draft|12=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=draft|12=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|12=draft|12=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|12=draft|12=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{draft categories|}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|12=draft|12=draft}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' (少初位) was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=draft|12=draft}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|12=draft|12=draft}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|ja|メンフィス|12=draft|12=draft}}, it represented the most important funerary festival in the {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|12=draft|12=draft}} necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=draft|12=draft}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|12=draft|12=draft}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the {{ill|Annals|ja|年代記|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of {{ill|Memphite|ja|メンフィス|12=draft|12=draft}} tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|12=draft|12=draft}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=draft|12=draft}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|12=draft|12=draft}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|12=draft|12=draft}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=draft|12=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=draft|12=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|12=draft|12=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|12=draft|12=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|12=draft|12=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=draft|12=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|12=draft|12=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|12=draft|12=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]]

''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|12=draft|12=draft}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' (少初位) was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=draft|12=draft}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|12=draft|12=draft}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=draft|12=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=draft|12=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|12=draft|12=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|12=draft|12=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|12=draft|12=draft}} I 1 sun. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=draft|12=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|12=draft|12=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|12=draft|12=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. ''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|12=draft|12=draft}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' (少初位) was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=draft|12=draft}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|12=draft|12=draft}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|ja|メンフィス|12=draft|12=draft}}, it represented the most important funerary festival in the {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|12=draft|12=draft}} necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=draft|12=draft}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|12=draft|12=draft}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the {{ill|Annals|ja|年代記|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of {{ill|Memphite|ja|メンフィス|12=draft|12=draft}} tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|12=draft|12=draft}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=draft|12=draft}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|12=draft|12=draft}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|12=draft|12=draft}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=draft|12=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=draft|12=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|12=draft|12=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|12=draft|12=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|12=draft|12=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=draft|12=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|12=draft|12=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|12=draft|12=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]]

''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' (少初位) was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|ja|メンフィス|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, it represented the most important funerary festival in the {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the {{ill|Annals|ja|年代記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of {{ill|Memphite|ja|メンフィス|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]]

Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} I 1 sun. {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' (少初位) was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|ja|メンフィス|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, it represented the most important funerary festival in the {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the {{ill|Annals|ja|年代記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of {{ill|Memphite|ja|メンフィス|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[Category:qq]]

Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} I 1 sun. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' (少初位) was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft}}, it represented the most important funerary festival in the {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft}} necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the {{ill|Annals|ja|年代記|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of {{ill|Memphite|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft}} tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|qq=draft}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|qq=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|qq=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|qq=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]]

Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|qq=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|qq=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|qq=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' (少初位) was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft}}, it represented the most important funerary festival in the {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft}} necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the {{ill|Annals|ja|年代記|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of {{ill|Memphite|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft}} tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|qq=draft}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|qq=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|qq=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|qq=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]]

Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|qq=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|qq=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|qq=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' (少初位) was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft}}, it represented the most important funerary festival in the {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft}} necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the {{ill|Annals|ja|年代記|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of {{ill|Memphite|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft}} tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|qq=draft}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|qq=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|qq=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|qq=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}}

Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|qq=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|qq=draft}} I 1 sun. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|qq=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages with German translation box]] {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' (少初位) was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft}}, it represented the most important funerary festival in the {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft}} necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the {{ill|Annals|ja|年代記|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of {{ill|Memphite|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft}} tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|qq=draft}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|qq=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|qq=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|qq=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}}

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] to [[Category:Completion tier 1]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' (少初位) was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft}}, it represented the most important funerary festival in the {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft}} necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the {{ill|Annals|ja|年代記|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of {{ill|Memphite|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft}} tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|qq=draft}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|qq=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|qq=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|qq=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]]

Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|qq=draft}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|qq=draft}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=draft}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|qq=draft}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]]

{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saqqara|qq=}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' (少初位) was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|qq=}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|qq=}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|qq=}} of {{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memphis (Egypt)|qq=}}, it represented the most important funerary festival in the {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saqqara|qq=}} necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the {{ill|Annals|ja|年代記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annals|qq=}} of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of {{ill|Memphite|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memphite|qq=}} tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thebes (Egypt)|qq=}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the {{ill|Djed|ja|ジェド柱|de|Djed-Pfeiler|Djed=Djed|lt=Djed pillar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Djed|qq=}}, which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sunrise|qq=}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|qq=}}" within the {{ill|Koiak|ja|コイアク|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koiak|qq=}}-{{ill|Isis rituals|ja|イシスの儀式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isis rituals|qq=}} replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sunrise|qq=}}, completing it with the first rays of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} I 1 sun. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thebes (Egypt)|qq=}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sunrise|qq=}}, completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Memphis (Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}
