{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saqqara|qq=}} }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|qq=}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|qq=}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|qq=}} of {{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memphis (Egypt)|qq=}}, it represented the most important funerary festival in the {{ill|Saqqara|ja|サッカラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saqqara|qq=}} necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the {{ill|Annals|ja|年代記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annals|qq=}} of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of {{ill|Memphite|ja|メンフィス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memphite|qq=}} tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=}} in {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|テーベ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thebes (Egypt)|qq=}}.Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before {{ill|sunrise|ja|日の出|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sunrise|qq=}}, completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|qq=}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|qq=}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|qq=}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Religiöses Fest (Altes Ägypten)]]

Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Religious_Festivals_of_Ancient_Egypt]]

revision.comment: Bot: merge Religious_Festivals_of_Ancient_Egypt → Ancient_Egyptian_festivals
revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|qq=}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|qq=}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|qq=}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]]

Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]]

On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国}}. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2298046]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[en:{{subst:FULLPAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2298046]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]]

On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q2298046]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Third Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727533}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=First Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51969174}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Second Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727503}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sokar Festival]][[Category:qq]]

''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209.
