Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Sokar Festival]]

revision.comment: Bot: add wikidata link templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q2298046]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Third Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727533}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=First Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51969174}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Second Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727503}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sokar Festival]][[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sokar Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Sokar Festival]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Sokar Festival]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}}

This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qid=" to "WD="
revision.text:
[[de:Sokar-Fest]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q2298046]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Third Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727533}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=First Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51969174}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Second Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727503}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}}

This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}}

revision.text:
[[de:Sokar-Fest]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2298046]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Third Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727533}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=First Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51969174}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Second Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727503}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}}

Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.comment: Appending wikidata link for Q2298046
revision.text:
[[de:Sokar-Fest]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2298046]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Third Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727533}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=First Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51969174}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Second Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727503}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). [[de:Sokar-Fest]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2298046]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Third Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727533}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=First Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51969174}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Second Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727503}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
[[de:Sokar-Fest]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2298046]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Third Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727533}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=First Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51969174}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Second Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727503}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}}

revision.comment: Consolidate multiple wikidata link templates into single one at end
revision.text:
[[de:Sokar-Fest]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2298046]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Third Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727533}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=First Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51969174}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Second Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727503}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2298046}}

revision.text:
[[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[de:Sokar-Fest]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2298046]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME2 = W3-O34:V31:D21-A40
Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Third Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727533}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=First Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51969174}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Second Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727503}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q2298046}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Heb-Sokar
''ḥb-Skr''
''Festival of Sokar'' |BILD1 = Saqqara map.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Overview of the necropolis of [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] }} The '''Sokar Festival''' was one of the oldest {{ill|Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja|古代エジプトの葬送習慣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian funerary practices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is attested as early as the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja|フルニン王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With {{ill|Sokar|ja|ソカル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the {{ill|god of the dead|ja|死者の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/god of the dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Memphis|Memphis (Egypt)]], it represented the most important funerary festival in the [[:en:Saqqara|Saqqara]] necropolis for kings of the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}, especially from the {{ill|Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第3王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第3王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Third Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727533}} onward. == Sokar Festival == === Early Period to Old Kingdom === In the [[:en:Chronicle|Annals]] of the {{ill|First Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第1王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第1王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=First Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q51969174}}, the Sokar Festival was always associated with the founding dates of [[:en:Memphis|Memphite]] tombs for royal gods. Only with the beginning of the {{ill|Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja|第2王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Dynasty of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第2王朝 (曖昧さ回避)|1=Second Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q79727503}} was it established as a "minor Sed festival," celebrated every six years from coronation during the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177819}}. On this occasion, the king appeared with the {{ill|Deshret|ja|下エジプトの王冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deshret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (crown of Lower Egypt).Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 56–57. The fifth Sokar Festival occurred within the context of the {{ill|Sed festival|ja|セド祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === New Kingdom to Greco-Roman Period === With the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|ja|新王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Memphite Sokar Festival was elevated to a nationwide annual funerary celebration, comparable to the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja|谷の祭り|Beautiful Festival of the Valley=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beautiful Festival of the Valley|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]].Jan Assmann: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Munich 2003, pp. 312–313. Several festival calendar entries attest to the celebrations of the Sokar Festival, which began at {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Day|fr|Journée_égyptienne_antique|de|Tag_(Altes_Ägypten)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|en:draft|lt=dawn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Day|qq=}} on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|ja|アヘト|Season of the Inundation=Season of the Inundation|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Inundation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV 26: {{Zitat|On Akhit IV 25, on this day of laying onions, on the night of the Goddess Festival and at the {{ill|morning light|ja|朝の光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning light|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Akhit IV 26, when one celebrates the Festival of Sokar in its annual feast.|Rites of celebration in the tomb of Khabekhenet (TT2)Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Mainz et al. 1950, pp. 91–92.}} The Sokar Festival ended on Akhit IV 30 with the symbolic raising of the [[:en:Djed|Djed pillar]], which the king began at dawn with the help of the priests shortly before [[:en:Sunrise|sunrise]], completing it with the first rays of the [[Season of the Emergence]] I 1 sun. This act symbolized the resurrection of the god Sokar and the mythological association with the king, who was to receive renewed strength for the years of reign to come through this ritual.Maria Carmela Betrò: ''Heilige Zeichen. 580 Ägyptische Hieroglyphen. Die ganze Welt der Pharaonen wird verständlich und lebendig.'' Marix, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-937715-33-9, p. 209. By the {{ill|Greco-Roman Egypt|ja|ギリシア・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greco-Roman Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the "{{ill|Festival of the Beating of the Earth|ja|地面をたたく祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of the Beating of the Earth|de|Fest_der_aufgehackten_Erde|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" within the [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]]-Isis rituals replaced the Sokar celebrations. == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten''. Special Edition. Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Sokar-Fest|version=201350031|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Sokar-Fest]] [[ja:ソカル祭]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q2298046}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Memphis, Egypt]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]
